$comment: Render-lint config for GitHub Pages-published markdown under
docs/audits/ and docs/self-improving/petri-bundle/README.md. The goal is to
catch syntax/structure issues that break Pages rendering — not
to enforce English-prose cosmetics.
Rules disabled (with rationale):
MD013 line-length: Korean engineer-essay tone runs long; we
rely on wrap-as-you-go rather than a hard column limit.
MD024 no-duplicate-heading: caveat blocks legitimately reuse
section headers like '### 본 root cause 의 actual' across
a long audit retrospective.
MD033 no-inline-html: README and audits embed <sub>, <kbd>,

and rare
for table-cell formatting.
MD041 first-line-h1: footnotes ([^credit]) appear before the
final H1 in some files because the audit footer pattern
keeps the cost note at the very bottom.
Rules kept on (with rationale):
MD001 heading-increment: ensures TOC renders predictably.
MD003 heading-style: enforces ATX-style # headings.
MD009 no-trailing-spaces: Pages renderer treats two trailing

spaces as
; surprises are bad.
MD010 no-hard-tabs: tabs render unpredictably in tables.
MD022 blanks-around-headings: missing blanks break heading
detection in some GFM renderers.
MD031 blanks-around-fences: same for code fences.
MD032 blanks-around-lists: same for lists.
MD040 fenced-code-language: code fences must declare a lang
so the bundle highlighter picks the right grammar.
MD055 table-pipe-style: caveat tables use leading/trailing
pipes; enforce so column alignment markers (---:) parse.
MD056 table-column-count: catches columns that fall out of
sync — the most common Pages render breakage.
MD058 blanks-around-tables: ensures table boundaries are
detected (common cause of un-rendered tables on Pages).
